text:
$PENG - PENGUIN SOLUTIONS IS AN AI-FACTORY PLATFORM AT A $2B MARKET CAP WITH BILLIONS IN REVENUE, $100M FCF, AND A FORWARD P/E UNDER 17×

Penguin Solutions, formerly SMART Global Holdings, integrates AI factory platforms across photonics, CPU, memory, and AI infrastructure. The stock closed Tuesday near $55 after running +80% in the last month — a tape that has noticed but a multiple that has not yet rerated to peers. $100M in free cash flow and a sub-17× forward P/E in a category where AI-factory comparable names trade at 30-50× revenue is the cleanest mispricing left in the $2B-cap AI-infra integrator bucket.

Mechanism: this is the picks-and-shovels expression of the same buildout driving NBIS, CRWV, and the neocloud cohort, but with positive free cash flow today instead of negative operating margins funded by debt. As hyperscalers and Tier-2 AI providers push integrators for prepackaged photonics + memory + power stacks, a profitable platform vendor at $2B mcap is a takeout candidate before it ever needs another capital raise.

@J5jayfive You don’t understand photonics, microchips and data processing inside out. It’s just a good time to ride these names. When the narrative flips people will get wiped because their conviction came from green candles on a screen.

Chip testing and Semi infra had some recent weakness and is recovering, offering a couple days of sideways digestion that could offer some nice entry points. Some areas of memory also digesting recent gains (MRAM) that be good r/r entries.

I see a lot of people saying Sivers is not a bottleneck so I wanted to write a quick thread on what actually makes something a bottleneck in photonics — and why $SIVE fits, even if many people say it doesn’t.

A lot of people get confused and think bottleneck is just “being the only supplier.” instead, It’s when a specific component is difficult and slow to replace at scale, because of technical complexity, long qualification times, or limited alternatives that meet the required performance.

Many people look at SIVE and say “it’s not a bottleneck” because:
- It doesn’t own its own high-volume fab
- There are other laser companies (Lumentum $LITE , Coherent $COHR , etc.)
- Hyperscalers usually multi-source

However, that misses the point:
SIVE’s position is narrow but very meaningful because very few suppliers can deliver the specific combination of power, wavelength stability, and thermal performance that several CPO architectures require.

Once these lasers are qualified into a system like $SIVE is with  (Ayar, Jabil ecosystem, $POET, etc.), switching is slow and risky. Qualification cycles in this layer are long. That friction is what creates a bottleneck — even without a full monopoly.

Here an image showing what makes $SIVE's lasers different from the others and hard to replace

text:
2. GPU와 스위치 성능은 계속 올라가는데

전기 신호 기반 연결은 거리와 발열 부담이 커지고 있어서 

광 I/O로 더 가까이 붙는 방식이 필요해진 것임. 

인텔은 4Tbps급 광 I/O 칩렛(chiplet, 기능별로 쪼갠 소형 반도체 블록)을 공개했고

엔비디아도 실리콘 포토닉스(silicon photonics, 반도체 위에서 빛으로 데이터를 전송하는 기술) 기반 네트워크를 차세대 AI 인프라 축으로 제시했음.

3. 가장 위쪽은 스위치 칩과 메인 프로세서 영역임. 

이 구간은 데이터 흐름을 직접 제어하는 두뇌 역할을 맡고

관련 기업은 $NVDA (엔비디아), $AVGO (브로드컴), $AMD (AMD), $MRVL (마벨), $GOOGL (알파벳)임. 

브로드컴은 51.2Tbps급 Bailly CPO 스위치를 고객사에 공급했다고 밝혔고

엔비디아는 Spectrum-X와 Quantum-X 포토닉스 스위치를 통해 대규모 GPU 연결 효율 개선을 강조하고 있음.

4. 그다음은 광엔진(optical engine, 전기 신호를 빛으로 바꾸고 다시 전기로 되돌리는 핵심 광모듈)임. 

CPO에서 사실상 성능과 전력 효율을 좌우하는 본체에 가까우며,

포토닉 집적회로(PIC, 광신호를 처리하는 집적칩), 변조기(modulator, 빛에 데이터를 실어 보내는 장치), 수광부가 함께 묶여 동작함. 

이 구간은 $TSM (TSMC), $INTC (인텔), $005930.KS (삼성전자), $NVDA (엔비디아), $AVGO (브로드컴)이 연결되며

TSMC는 COUPE 플랫폼을 통해 실리콘 포토닉스 패키징 양산 로드맵을 제시한 상태임.

(이어서 계속 🔗)

GPU와 스위치 성능은 계속 올라가는데 전기 신호 기반 연결은 거리와 발열 부담이 커지고 있어서 광 I/O로 더 가까이 붙는 방식이 필요해진 것임. 인텔은 4Tbps급 광 I/O 칩렛(chiplet, 기능별로 쪼갠 소형 반도체 블록)을 공개했고 엔비디아도 실리콘 포토닉스(silicon photonics, 반도체 위에서 빛으로 데이터를 전송하는 기술) 기반 네트워크를 차세대 AI 인프라 축으로 제시했음. 3. 가장 위쪽은 스위치 칩과 메인 프로세서 영역임. 이 구간은 데이터 흐름을 직접 제어하는 두뇌 역할을 맡고 관련 기업은 $NVDA (엔비디아), $AVGO (브로드컴), $AMD (AMD), $MRVL (마벨), $GOOGL (알파벳)임. 브로드컴은 51.2Tbps급 Bailly CPO 스위치를 고객사에 공급했다고 밝혔고 엔비디아는 Spectrum-X와 Quantum-X 포토닉스 스위치를 통해 대규모 GPU 연결 효율 개선을 강조하고 있음. 4. 그다음은 광엔진(optical engine, 전기 신호를 빛으로 바꾸고 다시 전기로 되돌리는 핵심 광모듈)임. CPO에서 사실상 성능과 전력 효율을 좌우하는 본체에 가까우며, 포토닉 집적회로(PIC, 광신호를 처리하는 집적칩), 변조기(modulator, 빛에 데이터를 실어 보내는 장치), 수광부가 함께 묶여 동작함. 이 구간은 $TSM (TSMC), $INTC (인텔), $005930.KS (삼성전자), $NVDA (엔비디아), $AVGO (브로드컴)이 연결되며 TSMC는 COUPE 플랫폼을 통해 실리콘 포토닉스 패키징 양산 로드맵을 제시한 상태임. text:
The rise of the photonic industry 

Marvell ($MRVL) and its role in the AI and photonics value chain ! 

Everyone talks about NVIDIA. Sometimes Micron. Occasionally Broadcom.

But very few people talk about the infrastructure that allows all these components to communicate with each other.

To understand why Marvell could become one of the most strategic companies in AI over the coming years, it’s worth taking a step back and looking at how an AI data center actually works.

🔹 How does an AI data center really work? 

Imagine an AI data center as a giant city filled with incredibly powerful brains.

🔹 1. GPUs: the engines of AI

Each GPU is like a Formula 1 race car for computation.

It can perform billions of calculations every second.

But a GPU by itself isn’t very useful.

🔹 2. GPUs need to communicate

Training today’s most advanced AI models requires thousands of GPUs working together.

The challenge?

The more GPUs you add, the more data they need to exchange.

Today, part of that communication still relies on electrical connections (copper), which consume a lot of power and generate a tremendous amount of heat.

🔹 3. The rise of optics

To move data faster and more efficiently, the industry is gradually replacing electricity with light.

Information travels through optical fibers at enormous speeds, with lower losses and significantly lower power consumption.

🔹 4. The real AI challenge

For years, compute power was the main bottleneck 

Today, connectivity is emerging as the next one with that simple question : 

How do you make tens of thousands of GPUs communicate efficiently?

That’s where Marvell comes in.

🔹 Marvell’s role

NVIDIA builds the brains.

Micron provides part of the memory.

Marvell builds the nervous system.

The company develops the technologies that allow all these processors to exchange data faster and more efficiently.

It is also one of the companies leading the transition toward next-generation optical architectures for massive AI clusters.

🔹 Why is this interesting?

As data centers become larger and larger, more value is shifting toward interconnects and networking.

GPUs get all the attention.

But without roads connecting them, even the fastest cars end up stuck in traffic.

That’s why Marvell keeps repeating the same message:

“The Future of AI Scaling Depends on Connectivity.”

🔹 The news that caught my attention

Ahead of its COMPUTEX keynote, Marvell showcased an impressive demonstration of its next-generation connectivity technology.

The company is working on so-called “co-packaged” architectures, where optics and copper are brought much closer to the compute chips themselves in order to reduce power consumption and eliminate future bandwidth bottlenecks.

The demonstration board connects two AI processors through 14 ultra-high-bandwidth CPC links, delivering nearly 90 Tb/s of total bandwidth.

The goal isn’t simply to go faster.

The goal is to enable future AI clusters with tens or even hundreds of thousands of GPUs to continue scaling without exploding power consumption.

When people look at AI today, they focus on GPUs.

When they look at AI tomorrow, they may need to pay just as much attention to what connects those GPUs together.

And that’s exactly where Marvell is positioning itself.

Ok now let’s look again at that post on X :

https://t.co/zB6TU3IjNy

Marvell expects near- and co-packaged connectivity ports to grow from fewer than 1 million units shipped in 2025 to more than 100 million units annually by 2030 !!! 

This is huge TAM !! 

If that forecast proves correct, we’re not just looking at a new product cycle — we’re potentially witnessing the beginning of a massive photonic infrastructure buildout. 

Just as GPUs became the backbone of the AI revolution, photonic connectivity could become one of the key beneficiaries of the next phase of AI scaling

The rise of photonic AI infra may just be beginning…. 

#Marvell #MRVL #Photonique #Optique #CPO #IA

It can perform billions of calculations every second. But a GPU by itself isn’t very useful. 🔹 2. GPUs need to communicate Training today’s most advanced AI models requires thousands of GPUs working together. The challenge? The more GPUs you add, the more data they need to exchange. Today, part of that communication still relies on electrical connections (copper), which consume a lot of power and generate a tremendous amount of heat. 🔹 3. The rise of optics To move data faster and more efficiently, the industry is gradually replacing electricity with light. Information travels through optical fibers at enormous speeds, with lower losses and significantly lower power consumption. 🔹 4. The real AI challenge For years, compute power was the main bottleneck Today, connectivity is emerging as the next one with that simple question : How do you make tens of thousands of GPUs communicate efficiently? That’s where Marvell comes in. 🔹 Marvell’s role NVIDIA builds the brains. Micron provides part of the memory. Marvell builds the nervous system. The company develops the technologies that allow all these processors to exchange data faster and more efficiently. It is also one of the companies leading the transition toward next-generation optical architectures for massive AI clusters. 🔹 Why is this interesting? As data centers become larger and larger, more value is shifting toward interconnects and networking. GPUs get all the attention. But without roads connecting them, even the fastest cars end up stuck in traffic. That’s why Marvell keeps repeating the same message: “The Future of AI Scaling Depends on Connectivity.” 🔹 The news that caught my attention Ahead of its COMPUTEX keynote, Marvell showcased an impressive demonstration of its next-generation connectivity technology. The company is working on so-called “co-packaged” architectures, where optics and copper are brought much closer to the compute chips themselves in order to reduce power consumption and eliminate future bandwidth bottlenecks. The demonstration board connects two AI processors through 14 ultra-high-bandwidth CPC links, delivering nearly 90 Tb/s of total bandwidth. The goal isn’t simply to go faster. The goal is to enable future AI clusters with tens or even hundreds of thousands of GPUs to continue scaling without exploding power consumption. When people look at AI today, they focus on GPUs. When they look at AI tomorrow, they may need to pay just as much attention to what connects those GPUs together. And that’s exactly where Marvell is positioning itself. Ok now let’s look again at that post on X : https://t.co/zB6TU3IjNy Marvell expects near- and co-packaged connectivity ports to grow from fewer than 1 million units shipped in 2025 to more than 100 million units annually by 2030 !!! This is huge TAM !! If that forecast proves correct, we’re not just looking at a new product cycle — we’re potentially witnessing the beginning of a massive photonic infrastructure buildout. Just as GPUs became the backbone of the AI revolution, photonic connectivity could become one of the key beneficiaries of the next phase of AI scaling The rise of photonic AI infra may just be beginning…. #Marvell #MRVL #Photonique #Optique #CPO #IA"}}}, "rest_id": "2060868475925488049", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "129", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

It's crazy to think about but it's perfectly possible that my existing positions make me tens of millions in the next several years 

$SIVE can 50x from here if photonics replace electronics  exactly when data centers and robots explode in numbers

$LPK can 50x if glass substrate goes into all next generation chips

$ACCON can 50x as many billions of micro-radars get installed into all vehicles, robots, public transit, smart homes, appliances

$GAPW-B can 50x as micro antennas go into billions of devices, robots and cars

$SILC can 50x as SmartNICS are installed into all data centers, edge AI and robots

$SEYE can 50x as all automatons and automatic systems  need to read and understand human behavior they encounter

$TRT can 50x as all the semiconductors for all this need to be tested

in which case I've already done everything I need to do for a lifetime.

BUT I will continue to grind and hustle hard every day, because I feel right now we have a golden opportunity to break through and become elite in the cyberpunk world we are building now. 

And the alterative in a cyberpunk high tech low life dystopia... Is not pretty.

text:
From a supply perspective, long-term storage is probably not as safe as photonics. Storage faces breakthrough supply from China’s two memory makers and has been through many cycles with proven expansion experience — it can shift the supply curve rightward faster. Optics, pointed out, lacks that scaling track record. Yields and process steps at each stage will fluctuate, so it’s much harder to ramp volume like storage.

Excellent analysis! This once again highlights Serenity’s investing timeline: while the memory investment cycle is already entering its mid-to-late stage, the optical/photonics cycle is just beginning to take off.
embedded_text: 从需求的角度看，存储和光学都没有问题，并且存储的需求和相对价值都更大，因而仅美光一家龙头的市值就超过了整个光学层。从供给的角度看，并且长期看，存储大概是没有光学安全的。

"从需求的角度看，存储和光学都没有问题，并且存储的需求和相对价值都更大，因而仅美光一家龙头的市值就超过了整个光学层。从供给的角度看，并且长期看，存储大概是没有光学安全的。 存储不仅面临中国两存的突破性供应，而且整个产业已周期过多轮，拥有丰富可靠的扩产经验，可以更快地使供给曲线左移。相比之下，如@ShanghaoJin 所言，光学缺乏扩产经验，各个环节良率等都会有波折，很难像存储一样上量。 因此我现在能确定的是： 1. 无论是存储还是光学，在中短期内需求烈度都要远大于供给扩张速度； 2. 存储的供给扩张速度要大于光学的供给扩张速度； 3. 2026-2028，存储的需求烈度大概是要大于光学的。因为存储需求受Training、Inference、Agent三重驱动，而光学需求结构单一，存储的需求动能更强。 4. 存储与光学相比，光学的需求/供给剪刀差更大。如上文所述，存储产业走过多轮周期，扩产经验丰富，叠加中国两存的强力加码，存储的供给曲线会更快的左移，而光学扩产更复杂，叠加技术升级的验证，良率会持续挑战，特别是硅光、CPO、光引擎和高速DSP会长期短缺，相比存储，其供给曲线更僵硬。 所以，当下就投资而言，我会更关注光互联产业链，因为市场已经充分理解“HBM缺货”，但对“未来几年光学供给可能比HBM更紧”这件事，定价还不充分。 如果未来AI集群从几十万卡扩张到数百万卡，那么网络成本占比会从目前约10%-15%进一步提升，光学层的重要性就会变得更大。届时光学可能不仅供需关系更好，连需求烈度本身都可能接近甚至超过存储。 未来2-3年，拿着存储，我不确定需求/供给剪刀差是否还足够大，始终会担心行业速度与中国产能，而拿着光，我的安全边际会更高，睡得也会更踏实。"}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 0, "favorite_count": 0, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, In summary, LC planar photonic devices, leveraging their unique soft-matter nature and multidimensional tunability, hold irreplaceable potential in integrated photonics. By synergistically advancing science, technology, and applications, they are poised to become a cornerstone of next-generation planar photonic platforms, driving transformative progress in optical field manipulation and accelerating the high-quality development of the global photonic information industry.

https://t.co/aZX5VlyeAZ

Absorption controls energy transfer between electromagnetic fields and matter, so it sets detection limits in spectroscopy, governs thermal emission, and informs the design of integrated photonics [1, 2]. Perfect absorption functions as a benchmark for coupling efficiency because it aligns impedance, material loss, and modal confinement to drive energy into the intended channel [3–5].

user_name: Thoughts on Healthcare Markets and Tech
text:
The bottleneck framing is right, but the sequence being described here stops one layer short of where the pressure actually lands in high-stakes deployment contexts like healthcare.

Compute, then interconnect, then photonics, yes. But the binding constraint for clinical AI isn't just moving data between GPUs faster. It's the energy cost of inference at the point where the economics have to close against reimbursement rates. Nvidia's GB200 delivers roughly 30 times better performance per watt on certain inference tasks versus the H100 (their own published benchmarks). That number matters more in a clinical workflow than raw throughput, because you're not optimizing for speed, you're optimizing for cost-per-query against what Medicare will actually pay.

Photonics solves a real problem for hyperscale data centers routing between racks. It's a meaningful unlock for training clusters. But the constraint that actually determines whether a community hospital can run continuous real-time ICU monitoring (processing vitals, imaging, lab values, and unstructured notes simultaneously across a whole floor) is compute-per-watt at the edge, not optical interconnect bandwidth inside a data center someone else operates.

The pattern this post describes is accurate. Where it gets complicated is assuming the next constraint is singular. Interconnect matters for centralized inference at scale. Energy efficiency matters for distributed inference at the care delivery layer. Those aren't the same problem, and the companies solving one won't automatically solve the other.

The X-ray analogy is useful here: the constraint wasn't generating electricity, it was delivering reliable current to a specific room in a specific building (which took decades longer than generating it centrally).

https://t.co/l8PmUCFrKc

Compute, then interconnect, then photonics, yes. But the binding constraint for clinical AI isn't just moving data between GPUs faster. It's the energy cost of inference at the point where the economics have to close against reimbursement rates. Nvidia's GB200 delivers roughly 30 times better performance per watt on certain inference tasks versus the H100 (their own published benchmarks). That number matters more in a clinical workflow than raw throughput, because you're not optimizing for speed, you're optimizing for cost-per-query against what Medicare will actually pay.

Photonics solves a real problem for hyperscale data centers routing between racks. It's a meaningful unlock for training clusters. But the constraint that actually determines whether a community hospital can run continuous real-time ICU monitoring (processing vitals, imaging, lab values, and unstructured notes simultaneously across a whole floor) is compute-per-watt at the edge, not optical interconnect bandwidth inside a data center someone else operates.

The pattern this post describes is accurate. Where it gets complicated is assuming the next constraint is singular. Interconnect matters for centralized inference at scale. Energy efficiency matters for distributed inference at the care delivery layer. Those aren't the same problem, and the companies solving one won't automatically solve the other.

The X-ray analogy is useful here: the constraint wasn't generating electricity, it was delivering reliable current to a specific room in a specific building (which took decades longer than generating it centrally).

Processors became faster, then memory became the", "id_str": "2060414438113788298", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "en", "quote_count": 1, "reply_count": 63, "retweet_count": 53, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "2414734874"}, "note_tweet": {"is_expandable": true, "note_tweet_results": {"result": {"entity_set": {"hashtags": [], "symbols": [], "urls": [{"display_url": "cnbc.com/2026/05/29/nvi…", "expanded_url": "https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/29/nvidia-photonics-investment-ai.html", "indices": [1882, 1905], "url": "https://t.co/aNxbVdttHz"}], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjA0MTQ0Mzc4Nzg4OTQ1OTI=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "Most people think technology revolutions are driven by breakthroughs.

In reality, they are driven by bottlenecks.

The history of computing is a story of constraints moving from one layer of the technology stack to another. Processors became faster, then memory became the constraint. Networks improved, then bandwidth became the constraint. Data exploded, then storage and infrastructure became the constraints.

AI is following the same pattern.

For the last few years, the industry's biggest challenge was compute. The race was about building larger models, training them faster, and deploying more powerful chips. Nvidia solved that challenge better than anyone else, which is why it became one of the most valuable companies in the world.

When one constraint gets solved, another emerges.

That is what makes Nvidia's recent investments in Lumentum, Coherent, Marvell, Corning, and Ayar Labs so interesting. These investments suggest the company is already looking beyond compute and focusing on what could become the next major constraint in AI infrastructure.

As AI systems scale from thousands of GPUs to millions, the challenge shifts from generating intelligence to moving massive amounts of information quickly, efficiently, and economically across increasingly complex systems. The faster AI advances, the more important this challenge becomes.

This is why the photonics story matters. It is far more than a story about a new technology. It is a story about identifying a future constraint before it slows down an entire ecosystem.

The companies creating the most value over the next decade may be the ones building smarter AI models. They may also be the ones solving the hidden infrastructure challenges that make large-scale AI possible in the first place.

The best investors look for emerging technologies.

The best companies look for emerging constraints.

user_name: Thoughts on Healthcare Markets and Tech
text:
The bottleneck framing is right, but the sequence being described here stops one layer short of where the pressure actually lands in high-stakes deployment contexts like healthcare.

Compute, then interconnect, then photonics, yes. But the binding constraint for clinical AI isn't just moving data between GPUs faster. It's the energy cost of inference at the point where the economics have to close against reimbursement rates. Nvidia's GB200 delivers roughly 30 times better performance per watt on certain inference tasks versus the H100 (their own published benchmarks). That number matters more in a clinical workflow than raw throughput, because you're not optimizing for speed, you're optimizing for cost-per-query against what Medicare will actually pay.

Photonics solves a real problem for hyperscale data centers routing between racks. It's a meaningful unlock for training clusters. But the constraint that actually determines whether a community hospital can run continuous real-time ICU monitoring (processing vitals, imaging, lab values, and unstructured notes simultaneously across a whole floor) is compute-per-watt at the edge, not optical interconnect bandwidth inside a data center someone else operates.

The pattern this post describes is accurate. Where it gets complicated is assuming the next constraint is singular. Interconnect matters for centralized inference at scale. Energy efficiency matters for distributed inference at the care delivery layer. Those aren't the same problem, and the companies solving one won't automatically solve the other.

The X-ray analogy is useful here: the constraint wasn't generating electricity, it was delivering reliable current to a specific room in a specific building (which took decades longer than generating it centrally).

text:
What is happening with $SIVE Sivers Semiconductors?

In this cross-post from my YouTube channel by the same name, I explain all the drama with the Swedish photonics company and how it became the most loved and hated stock in the world and what role the infamous trader @aleabitoreddit played.

Idę zaraz spać, więc nie będę się rozpisywał, ale bardzo się mylisz.

Cała* polska składka ESA wraca do polskich firm. To jest warunek współpracy.

Mamy ICEYE, mamy Creotech, mamy Scanway, mamy Vigo Photonics i cały szereg innych firm. Creotech buduje pierwszą europejską sondę kosmiczną od kilkudziesięciu lat.

Polska już teraz ma w kosmosie więcej satelitów rozpoznawczych niż większość krajów NATO.

Masz tu artykuł sprzed pół roku:
https://t.co/cs0kkZws3I

text:
Since $MRVL was up +32%, let me introduce you Taiwan’s Most Direct $MRVL related Stock: LuxNet Corporation 華星光（4979):

Marvell’s push into high-speed Optical DSPs and silicon photonics/CPO solutions for AI data centers has created clear demand for supporting photonic components. LuxNet stands out because of its specific role and long-term relationship with Marvell.

Here’s why the linkage is considered particularly direct:

1.  Critical Photonic Component Supplier
 LuxNet produces InP-based CW Lasers, which serve as the essential external light source in silicon photonics and CPO architectures. These lasers are a key part of enabling Marvell’s advanced Optical DSP platforms.

2.  Long-Standing Partnership The relationship goes back to Marvell’s acquisition of Inphi. 

LuxNet has been a manufacturing and development partner for Marvell’s high-speed optical transceiver modules and continues to work with the company on next-generation solutions.

3.  Frequently Cited in Market Coverage
 When Marvell reports progress in optical or silicon photonics, or when Nvidia deepens cooperation with Marvell, LuxNet is regularly included in analyst and media lists of the most direct Taiwan beneficiaries.
 Example: Recent coverage on Nvidia-Marvell silicon photonics collaboration highlighted LuxNet as part of the Taiwan optical supply chain that stands to benefit.
4.  Higher Specificity Than Most Peers Unlike broader semiconductor or foundry plays, LuxNet’s involvement is more component-specific and tied directly to Marvell’s optical product roadmap, making the supply chain connection more visible and targeted.

This combination of technical role, historical partnership, and consistent market recognition is why LuxNet is often viewed as one of the clearest $MRVL supply chain exposures in Taiwan.

#MRVL #LuxNet #4979 #CPO #SiliconPhotonics

text:
Wind Financial Morning Post: June 3, 2026

Market Brief

The State Council issues the "15th Five-Year Plan for Accelerating Agricultural and Rural Modernization"

China achieves significant breakthrough in optical communication technology

Shanghai establishes a new landmark for the global asset management center: scale to reach RMB 5.5 trillion by 2030

Prospects for U.S.-Iran negotiations remain uncertain, while applications for transit permits through the Strait of Hormuz have opened

The United States lowers import tariffs on selected steel, aluminum, and copper products, and plans to impose a new 25% tariff on Brazil

Global stock markets rose Tuesday, with U.S. markets hitting new highs and South Korea’s market entering the world’s top six largest securities markets

Tiger Brokers: Suspends new position opening and additional positioning for existing investor accounts in all domestic Chinese instruments effective June 12

Microsoft releases seven new AI models to create an integrated “thinking + programming” agent ecosystem

Top Stories

Shanghai has unveiled 21 measures to deepen its construction as a global asset management center, aiming for total asset management scale of 5.4 trillion yuan by 2030, accounting for one-third of the national total. The city will support listings and mergers of technology firms with strong innovation profiles; accelerate the launch of LNG futures and options; prepare for power and computing power futures; promote the listing of Star 50 Index, Shenzhen 100 Index, ChiNext Index futures and options, and government bond options; enhance mechanisms such as “Shanghai-Hong Kong Stock Connect,” “China-Europe Connect,” and “Bond Connect”; and steadily expand institutional openness in the gold market.

Tiger Brokers announced that, to comply with regulatory requirements during the cross-border securities business consolidation period, it will adjust services for existing investor accounts in mainland China starting June 12. Measures include suspending new positions and additional purchases across all securities products, permitting only sell and close-out transactions; and halting inbound fund transfers while maintaining outbound transfer functionality. The company reported first-quarter revenue of $155 million, up 26.3% year-over-year, and operating profit of $48 million, up 17.5%.

According to Iranian sources, the system for applying for vessel transit permits through the Strait of Hormuz has been fully opened, allowing global shipowners and captains to submit applications. Upon approval, vessels will receive transit authorization. Iranian media reported that information exchanges between Iran and the U.S. have been suspended for several days, despite Western media and officials claiming ongoing dialogue. U.S. President Trump stated that talks with Iran continue. U.S. Secretary of State Rubio affirmed that negotiations are still underway but expressed uncertainty over a potential agreement.

Microsoft unveiled seven new AI models at the Build 2026 developer conference, covering full-stack capabilities in reasoning, coding, vision, speech, and multimodal functions. Its flagship reasoning model, MAI-Thinking-1, matched Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 4.6 on coding benchmarks. The 5-billion-parameter coding model, MAI-Code-1-Flash, targets the GitHub ecosystem. Microsoft also introduced Web IQ, a search engine defined as a “network intelligence layer for AI agents.”

China Macro & Policy

The State Council issued the “15th Five-Year Plan for Accelerating Agricultural and Rural Modernization,” outlining major projects including high-standard farmland construction and cold-chain logistics facilities. The plan promotes diversified food development and the “AI+” agriculture initiative, and identifies key policy measures to cultivate emerging and future industries in agriculture.

National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) Director Zheng Zhijie convened a meeting with state-owned enterprises to discuss deepening SOE reform, advancing the national unified market, promoting green and low-carbon development, and ensuring energy security. Zheng emphasized enhancing the technological innovation, industrial control, and security support functions of state-owned enterprises.

The Ministry of Finance recently allocated 99.9 billion yuan in childcare subsidies for 2026, a 10.6% increase from last year, to support local governments in providing subsidies to eligible infants. Total fiscal subsidies for the year are projected to reach approximately 110 billion yuan.

The People’s Bank of China reported that in May, standing lending facility (SLF) net injections amounted to 900 million yuan, pledged supplementary lending (PSL) net withdrawals totaled 154.5 billion yuan, and other structural monetary policy tools net injected 112.9 billion yuan; open market treasury purchases resulted in a net injection of 50 billion yuan.

China’s first online tax-refund-for-overseas-tourists store has officially launched in Beijing, alongside the simultaneous activation of centralized tax-refund points in the city’s hotel sector. Overseas visitors can now process tax refunds directly at hotels after online purchases, expanding the scope of tax-refund services.

China Equities Markets

A-share markets climbed modestly, with the SSE Index rising 0.43% to 4,075.10, the Shenzhen Component Index up 1.63%, and the ChiNext Index jumping 2.66%. The Wind All China Index gained 0.75%, with total market turnover reaching 2.81 trillion yuan. The AI computing hardware supply chain strengthened again, with CPO and optical fiber sectors surging. Components, AI PCs, PCBs, and MLCC concepts advanced. Coal stocks maintained strength, while film and theater, and short-form video game concepts declined.

The Hang Seng Index closed up 2.52% at 26,038.32, the Hang Seng Tech Index surged 4.72%, and the Hang Seng China Enterprises Index rose 3%. Technology stocks rallied broadly, with Tencent Holdings soaring over 10%. New energy vehicles and non-ferrous metals led gains, while AI applications and coal stocks declined. Net inflows via the Southbound Stock Connect totaled 2.2 billion Hong Kong dollars, with Tencent Holdings experiencing 2.1 billion Hong Kong dollars in net outflows.

Fund managers led by Jin Zicai announced that to ensure stable fund operations and protect investor interests, subscription limits for multiple funds — including Caichong Growth Optimum, Caichong Value Momentum, Caichong Integrated Circuit Industry, and Caichong Quality Selection — have been lowered to 1,000 yuan.

The Shanghai Stock Exchange website shows that Yushu Technology’s STAR Market IPO status has changed to “submitted for registration.” Yushu Technology’s market director Huang Jiawei revealed that the company’s new-generation humanoid robot reference design, H2 Plus, developed jointly with NVIDIA, will launch in the second half of the year.

China Industry News

China has achieved a major breakthrough in optical communications. The world’s first S+C+L triple-band (short, conventional, and long wavelength) ultra-low-loss multi-core optical cable line has been officially commissioned in Qingdao, Shandong. The single-fiber capacity exceeds five times that of traditional fibers, fully meeting future demands for AI computing and terabit-per-second ultra-high-speed transmission.

Four ministries have launched the “Sword Net 2026” campaign to combat online copyright infringement, with a focus on AI-related copyright issues. The initiative targets violations such as “remixing,” “plagiarism,” and deepfake content in large model training datasets.

According to Centaline Property Research, 141,000 second-hand residential units were sold in 20 cities in May, up 19.3% year-over-year, with the growth rate expanding 6.3 percentage points from April. Beijing and Shanghai recorded 16,000 and 28,000 second-hand home sales respectively, up 12.1% and 30.9% year-over-year, marking the highest May levels in nearly five years. The average second-hand home price across 100 cities fell 0.32% month-over-month, while new home prices rose 0.16%.

Guangzhou’s Nansha District unveiled “Ten Measures” for real estate policy, launching an “old-home-for-new-home” initiative where proceeds from selling an old property can be directly applied toward a new purchase. A subsidy of 1% of the new home’s total price — capped at 20,000 yuan per unit — will be provided, and this can be stacked with municipal subsidies.

The China Passenger Car Association estimated that domestic new energy passenger vehicle wholesale sales reached 1.36 million units in May, up over 11% year-over-year and month-over-month. According to the China Automobile Dealers Association, the automobile consumption index stood at 81 in May, slightly higher than April, suggesting a continued steady upward trend in overall sales for June.

Fintech

Shanghai’s guidelines on deepening its global asset management center status encourage asset managers to leverage AI, blockchain, and big data technologies to accelerate intelligent investment research and advisory services. The city will support the establishment of AI application pilot bases for asset management.

The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission issued a circular requiring licensed institutions — particularly online brokers and virtual asset platforms — to strengthen cybersecurity measures to counter emerging threats driven by advanced AI models.

Corporate News & Updates

Sources indicate Tencent has completed prototype testing of its WeChat AI agent and will initiate compliance review procedures as early as this month. Users will be able to use the agent to trigger hundreds of mini-programs for daily tasks such as food ordering. The project has been designated the highest strategic priority. Tencent Cloud has reduced pricing for the DeepSeek-V4 series models by up to 97.5%.

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang stated that agent-based AI will fundamentally reshape all AI applications and represent the most important computing transformation over the next decade. Huang also said Marvell Technology will become the next trillion-dollar company. NVIDIA announced full-scale mass production of its NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet silicon photonics technology based on CPO.

Samsung Electronics unveiled its eighth-generation high-bandwidth memory chip, HBM5, and publicly introduced its HPB cooling solution for the first time. Chief Technology Officer Jae-Hyung Song confirmed that HPB technology has been validated on HBM4E chips and will be commercially deployed with HBM5.

Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger, in his Computex 2026 keynote, said agentic AI is reshaping computing demand, and future AI infrastructure will enter a heterogeneous era of co-development among CPUs, GPUs, ASICs, and custom chips. Demand for CPUs in data centers is expected to re-enter a growth cycle.

SK Group Chairman Choi Tae-won said the global shortage of memory chips may persist until 2030, and the company is increasing capital expenditures to bridge the supply-demand gap. SK Hynix plans to double its wafer capacity over the next five years.

ByteDance’s DouBao large model is expected to launch paid content in late June, with plans to integrate e-commerce functions in the third quarter to drive traffic to Douyin Mall. Longer-term operational strategies are not ruled out.

Anthropic announced the expansion of its Glasswing initiative to 150 new partner organizations across more than 15 countries. These institutions will use the Mythos tool to identify software vulnerabilities. New partners span energy, water, healthcare, telecommunications, and hardware industries.

OpenAI released a suite of new plugins for its Codex agent, covering public equity investment, banking, and sales use cases, and plans to introduce legal and corporate finance functions soon. OpenAI also intends to integrate Codex into ChatGPT.

Alibaba Cloud’s Qwen model launched Qwen3.7-Plus, a multimodal large model capable of understanding images and videos, performing deep reasoning, self-programming, tool invocation, validation, and autonomous iteration. It can complete complex, long-horizon tasks such as replicating smartphone apps and desktop software with a single command.

Global Macro & Policy

U.S. President Trump signed an executive order temporarily adjusting import tariffs on certain steel, aluminum, and copper products, effective June 8 through December 31, 2027. Import tariffs on harvesters and other agricultural machinery will be reduced from 25% to 15%, and the domestic content threshold for imported steel, aluminum, and copper will be lowered from 95% to 85%.

The United States plans to impose 25% punitive tariffs on a range of Brazilian imports, including electronic payment services, preferential tariff treatment, intellectual property protections, and ethanol market access. Exemptions include beef, coffee, rare earth minerals, aircraft and aircraft parts, crude oil, fertilizers, and numerous fruits and nuts.

The Canadian government has proposed to the U.S. and Mexico that the USMCA be renewed for 16 years to avoid triggering the agreement’s mandatory annual review process. Under USMCA provisions, the three nations are scheduled to conduct their first joint review in July. The agreement, signed in 2018 and effective since July 2020, is set to expire in 2036.

U.S. officials are reportedly discussing whether to deploy nuclear weapons in additional NATO European countries. The U.S. already deploys nuclear-capable bombers in Belgium, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Turkey, and the UK, and has signaled openness to additional deployments.

Hawkish voices within the Federal Reserve are intensifying. Cleveland Fed President Harker said while maintaining current rates remains appropriate, persistent inflation data may soon necessitate policy action — including potential further rate hikes — to counter elevated inflation risks.

Labor market resilience continues to bolster Fed hawkish expectations. U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics data showed job openings rose to 7.62 million in April, the highest level in two years and well above the 6.87 million forecast. Layoffs fell to 1.692 million, significantly below expectations.

The European Union plans to impose strict eligibility criteria for cloud computing services in key public procurement projects, potentially excluding Amazon, Microsoft, and Google. The proposal aims to reduce EU dependence on U.S. tech products and promote domestic industry development.

Eurostat preliminary data showed euro area CPI rose 3.2% year-over-year in May, up from 3% in April, reinforcing market expectations of a modest ECB rate hike in June.

South Korea’s central bank is poised to raise rates. Statistics Korea reported that May CPI rose 3.1% year-over-year, the largest increase since March 2024. Core CPI increased 2.5%, the highest since February 2024.

The World Meteorological Organization released a report indicating most climate models predict this El Niño will develop into at least a moderate event and may intensify further. Seasonal forecasts suggest most regions will experience above-average temperatures in the coming months.

Global Equity Markets

U.S. major indices closed slightly higher: the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.45% to 51,307.79, the S&P 500 gained 0.13% to 7,609.78, and the Nasdaq Composite edged up 0.03% to 27,093.90 — all closing at record highs. Cisco rose over 5%, Caterpillar gained more than 5%, leading the Dow. The Wind US Magnificent Seven Index declined 1.05%, with Microsoft down over 4% and Google down nearly 4%. The Nasdaq China Golden Dragon Index rose 1.83%, with Canadian Solar up over 12% and Hesai Group up over 8%. Traders closely monitored developments in U.S.-Iran relations and major tech stock performance.

European indices closed higher: Germany’s DAX rose 0.48% to 25,124.17, France’s CAC 40 gained 0.77% to 8,209.09, and the UK’s FTSE 100 climbed 0.33% to 10,373.51. Progress in ceasefire negotiations between Iran and the U.S. lifted risk appetite, but Brent crude breached $95 per barrel, maintaining upward pressure on inflation expectations.

Asia-Pacific markets closed mixed. South Korea’s KOSPI swung sharply — initially surging before plunging more than 3% — and ultimately closed up 0.15% at 8,801.49, setting a new record. South Korea’s total market capitalization surpassed India’s, becoming the world’s sixth-largest stock market. Japan’s Nikkei 225 fell 0.3% to 66,734.24. India’s SENSEX rose 0.52% to 74,649.84.

Andrew Left, founder of the renowned short-selling firm Citron Research, was convicted by a U.S. court of stock manipulation and may face decades in prison. The case could mark a landmark moment for short-sellers globally and reshape regulatory boundaries for short reports and market commentary.

Data from India’s National Securities Depository Limited showed that as of June 1, foreign institutional investors’ net investment in Indian equities totaled 7.3 trillion rupees — the lowest since 2016. Global fund ownership in Indian equities has declined from nearly 20% a decade ago to 15% today.

Bonds

China’s interbank bond market traded in a narrow range, with yields on major government bonds moving slightly in different directions. Treasury futures rallied slightly at the close, with the 10-year contract up 0.06%. Liquidity conditions returned to ample levels as the PBOC conducted 200 million yuan in reverse repo operations, resulting in a net withdrawal of 248.8 billion yuan.

U.S. Treasury yields were mixed: the 2-year yield rose 1.24 basis points to 4.039%, the 3-year yield rose 0.59 basis points to 4.085%, the 5-year yield held steady at 4.163%, the 10-year yield fell 1.18 basis points to 4.439%, and the 30-year yield declined 1.21 basis points to 4.957%.

Commodities

U.S. crude oil futures rose 1.33% to $93.39 per barrel; Brent crude gained 1.01% to $95.94 per barrel. U.S. API crude inventories fell sharply, exceeding market expectations, while geopolitical tensions in the Middle East — including Iran’s suspension of U.S.-Iran talks and its plan to block the Strait of Hormuz — heightened concerns over supply disruptions. The strait handles roughly 20% of global oil shipments, and global crude inventories remain at low levels, jointly supporting higher prices.

International precious metals futures generally rose: COMEX gold futures gained 0.29% to $4,519.20 per ounce, and COMEX silver futures rose 0.25% to $75.44 per ounce. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East lifted safe-haven demand, while rising U.S. and European rate expectations weighed on market sentiment, creating a tug-of-war.

Most base metals on the LME rose: LME tin climbed 2.88% to $58,280 per ton, LME lead gained 1.52% to $2,037 per ton, LME zinc rose 1.48% to $3,631 per ton, LME aluminum advanced 1.20% to $3,760.50 per ton, LME copper increased 1.10% to $13,983.50 per ton, while LME nickel edged down 0.19% to $19,215 per ton.

The European Central Bank reported that as of the end of 2025, gold accounted for 27% of global official reserve assets, surpassing U.S. Treasuries, which fell to 22%. Gold has become the world’s largest official reserve asset.

Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) revealed plans to construct a refined products pipeline to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. The company is also exploring a new east-west crude oil trunk line that would allow other Gulf producers to route exports through UAE territory, avoiding the strait.

FX & Currency Markets

Onshore renminbi closed at 6.7622 against the U.S. dollar at 16:30, up 28 basis points from the previous close, and settled at 6.7650 in the night session. The PBOC’s mid-rate was set at 6.8187, a 20-basis-point depreciation from the prior day.

At the close of New York trading, the U.S. Dollar Index rose 0.03% to 99.22. Non-U.S. currencies were mixed: the euro fell 0.01% to 1.1631, the pound rose 0.07% to 1.3464, the Australian dollar rose 0.27% to 0.7180, the dollar rose 0.16% against the yen to 159.9295, the dollar fell 0.01% against the Canadian dollar to 1.3839, the dollar rose 0.08% against the Swiss franc to 0.7873, and the offshore renminbi gained 23 basis points to 6.7629.

Japan’s authorities issued a strong signal after record-sized foreign exchange interventions proved ineffective. Finance Minister Kato said the government stands ready to take appropriate action on exchange rates if necessary.

Market Brief

The State Council issues the "15th Five-Year Plan for Accelerating Agricultural and Rural Modernization"

China achieves significant breakthrough in optical communication technology

Shanghai establishes a new landmark for the global asset management center: scale to reach RMB 5.5 trillion by 2030

Prospects for U.S.-Iran negotiations remain uncertain, while applications for transit permits through the Strait of Hormuz have opened

The United States lowers import tariffs on selected steel, aluminum, and copper products, and plans to impose a new 25% tariff on Brazil

Global stock markets rose Tuesday, with U.S. markets hitting new highs and South Korea’s market entering the world’s top six largest securities markets

Tiger Brokers: Suspends new position opening and additional positioning for existing investor accounts in all domestic Chinese instruments effective June 12

Microsoft releases seven new AI models to create an integrated “thinking + programming” agent ecosystem

Top Stories

Shanghai has unveiled 21 measures to deepen its construction as a global asset management center, aiming for total asset management scale of 5.4 trillion yuan by 2030, accounting for one-third of the national total. The city will support listings and mergers of technology firms with strong innovation profiles; accelerate the launch of LNG futures and options; prepare for power and computing power futures; promote the listing of Star 50 Index, Shenzhen 100 Index, ChiNext Index futures and options, and government bond options; enhance mechanisms such as “Shanghai-Hong Kong Stock Connect,” “China-Europe Connect,” and “Bond Connect”; and steadily expand institutional openness in the gold market.

Tiger Brokers announced that, to comply with regulatory requirements during the cross-border securities business consolidation period, it will adjust services for existing investor accounts in mainland China starting June 12. Measures include suspending new positions and additional purchases across all securities products, permitting only sell and close-out transactions; and halting inbound fund transfers while maintaining outbound transfer functionality. The company reported first-quarter revenue of $155 million, up 26.3% year-over-year, and operating profit of $48 million, up 17.5%.

According to Iranian sources, the system for applying for vessel transit permits through the Strait of Hormuz has been fully opened, allowing global shipowners and captains to submit applications. Upon approval, vessels will receive transit authorization. Iranian media reported that information exchanges between Iran and the U.S. have been suspended for several days, despite Western media and officials claiming ongoing dialogue. U.S. President Trump stated that talks with Iran continue. U.S. Secretary of State Rubio affirmed that negotiations are still underway but expressed uncertainty over a potential agreement.

Microsoft unveiled seven new AI models at the Build 2026 developer conference, covering full-stack capabilities in reasoning, coding, vision, speech, and multimodal functions. Its flagship reasoning model, MAI-Thinking-1, matched Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 4.6 on coding benchmarks. The 5-billion-parameter coding model, MAI-Code-1-Flash, targets the GitHub ecosystem. Microsoft also introduced Web IQ, a search engine defined as a “network intelligence layer for AI agents.”

China Macro & Policy

The State Council issued the “15th Five-Year Plan for Accelerating Agricultural and Rural Modernization,” outlining major projects including high-standard farmland construction and cold-chain logistics facilities. The plan promotes diversified food development and the “AI+” agriculture initiative, and identifies key policy measures to cultivate emerging and future industries in agriculture.

National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) Director Zheng Zhijie convened a meeting with state-owned enterprises to discuss deepening SOE reform, advancing the national unified market, promoting green and low-carbon development, and ensuring energy security. Zheng emphasized enhancing the technological innovation, industrial control, and security support functions of state-owned enterprises.

The Ministry of Finance recently allocated 99.9 billion yuan in childcare subsidies for 2026, a 10.6% increase from last year, to support local governments in providing subsidies to eligible infants. Total fiscal subsidies for the year are projected to reach approximately 110 billion yuan.

The People’s Bank of China reported that in May, standing lending facility (SLF) net injections amounted to 900 million yuan, pledged supplementary lending (PSL) net withdrawals totaled 154.5 billion yuan, and other structural monetary policy tools net injected 112.9 billion yuan; open market treasury purchases resulted in a net injection of 50 billion yuan.

China’s first online tax-refund-for-overseas-tourists store has officially launched in Beijing, alongside the simultaneous activation of centralized tax-refund points in the city’s hotel sector. Overseas visitors can now process tax refunds directly at hotels after online purchases, expanding the scope of tax-refund services.

China Equities Markets

A-share markets climbed modestly, with the SSE Index rising 0.43% to 4,075.10, the Shenzhen Component Index up 1.63%, and the ChiNext Index jumping 2.66%. The Wind All China Index gained 0.75%, with total market turnover reaching 2.81 trillion yuan. The AI computing hardware supply chain strengthened again, with CPO and optical fiber sectors surging. Components, AI PCs, PCBs, and MLCC concepts advanced. Coal stocks maintained strength, while film and theater, and short-form video game concepts declined.

The Hang Seng Index closed up 2.52% at 26,038.32, the Hang Seng Tech Index surged 4.72%, and the Hang Seng China Enterprises Index rose 3%. Technology stocks rallied broadly, with Tencent Holdings soaring over 10%. New energy vehicles and non-ferrous metals led gains, while AI applications and coal stocks declined. Net inflows via the Southbound Stock Connect totaled 2.2 billion Hong Kong dollars, with Tencent Holdings experiencing 2.1 billion Hong Kong dollars in net outflows.

Fund managers led by Jin Zicai announced that to ensure stable fund operations and protect investor interests, subscription limits for multiple funds — including Caichong Growth Optimum, Caichong Value Momentum, Caichong Integrated Circuit Industry, and Caichong Quality Selection — have been lowered to 1,000 yuan.

The Shanghai Stock Exchange website shows that Yushu Technology’s STAR Market IPO status has changed to “submitted for registration.” Yushu Technology’s market director Huang Jiawei revealed that the company’s new-generation humanoid robot reference design, H2 Plus, developed jointly with NVIDIA, will launch in the second half of the year.

China Industry News

China has achieved a major breakthrough in optical communications. The world’s first S+C+L triple-band (short, conventional, and long wavelength) ultra-low-loss multi-core optical cable line has been officially commissioned in Qingdao, Shandong. The single-fiber capacity exceeds five times that of traditional fibers, fully meeting future demands for AI computing and terabit-per-second ultra-high-speed transmission.

Four ministries have launched the “Sword Net 2026” campaign to combat online copyright infringement, with a focus on AI-related copyright issues. The initiative targets violations such as “remixing,” “plagiarism,” and deepfake content in large model training datasets.

According to Centaline Property Research, 141,000 second-hand residential units were sold in 20 cities in May, up 19.3% year-over-year, with the growth rate expanding 6.3 percentage points from April. Beijing and Shanghai recorded 16,000 and 28,000 second-hand home sales respectively, up 12.1% and 30.9% year-over-year, marking the highest May levels in nearly five years. The average second-hand home price across 100 cities fell 0.32% month-over-month, while new home prices rose 0.16%.

Guangzhou’s Nansha District unveiled “Ten Measures” for real estate policy, launching an “old-home-for-new-home” initiative where proceeds from selling an old property can be directly applied toward a new purchase. A subsidy of 1% of the new home’s total price — capped at 20,000 yuan per unit — will be provided, and this can be stacked with municipal subsidies.

The China Passenger Car Association estimated that domestic new energy passenger vehicle wholesale sales reached 1.36 million units in May, up over 11% year-over-year and month-over-month. According to the China Automobile Dealers Association, the automobile consumption index stood at 81 in May, slightly higher than April, suggesting a continued steady upward trend in overall sales for June.

Fintech

Shanghai’s guidelines on deepening its global asset management center status encourage asset managers to leverage AI, blockchain, and big data technologies to accelerate intelligent investment research and advisory services. The city will support the establishment of AI application pilot bases for asset management.

The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission issued a circular requiring licensed institutions — particularly online brokers and virtual asset platforms — to strengthen cybersecurity measures to counter emerging threats driven by advanced AI models.

Corporate News & Updates

Sources indicate Tencent has completed prototype testing of its WeChat AI agent and will initiate compliance review procedures as early as this month. Users will be able to use the agent to trigger hundreds of mini-programs for daily tasks such as food ordering. The project has been designated the highest strategic priority. Tencent Cloud has reduced pricing for the DeepSeek-V4 series models by up to 97.5%.

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang stated that agent-based AI will fundamentally reshape all AI applications and represent the most important computing transformation over the next decade. Huang also said Marvell Technology will become the next trillion-dollar company. NVIDIA announced full-scale mass production of its NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet silicon photonics technology based on CPO.

Samsung Electronics unveiled its eighth-generation high-bandwidth memory chip, HBM5, and publicly introduced its HPB cooling solution for the first time. Chief Technology Officer Jae-Hyung Song confirmed that HPB technology has been validated on HBM4E chips and will be commercially deployed with HBM5.

Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger, in his Computex 2026 keynote, said agentic AI is reshaping computing demand, and future AI infrastructure will enter a heterogeneous era of co-development among CPUs, GPUs, ASICs, and custom chips. Demand for CPUs in data centers is expected to re-enter a growth cycle.

SK Group Chairman Choi Tae-won said the global shortage of memory chips may persist until 2030, and the company is increasing capital expenditures to bridge the supply-demand gap. SK Hynix plans to double its wafer capacity over the next five years.

ByteDance’s DouBao large model is expected to launch paid content in late June, with plans to integrate e-commerce functions in the third quarter to drive traffic to Douyin Mall. Longer-term operational strategies are not ruled out.

Anthropic announced the expansion of its Glasswing initiative to 150 new partner organizations across more than 15 countries. These institutions will use the Mythos tool to identify software vulnerabilities. New partners span energy, water, healthcare, telecommunications, and hardware industries.

OpenAI released a suite of new plugins for its Codex agent, covering public equity investment, banking, and sales use cases, and plans to introduce legal and corporate finance functions soon. OpenAI also intends to integrate Codex into ChatGPT.

Alibaba Cloud’s Qwen model launched Qwen3.7-Plus, a multimodal large model capable of understanding images and videos, performing deep reasoning, self-programming, tool invocation, validation, and autonomous iteration. It can complete complex, long-horizon tasks such as replicating smartphone apps and desktop software with a single command.

Global Macro & Policy

U.S. President Trump signed an executive order temporarily adjusting import tariffs on certain steel, aluminum, and copper products, effective June 8 through December 31, 2027. Import tariffs on harvesters and other agricultural machinery will be reduced from 25% to 15%, and the domestic content threshold for imported steel, aluminum, and copper will be lowered from 95% to 85%.

The United States plans to impose 25% punitive tariffs on a range of Brazilian imports, including electronic payment services, preferential tariff treatment, intellectual property protections, and ethanol market access. Exemptions include beef, coffee, rare earth minerals, aircraft and aircraft parts, crude oil, fertilizers, and numerous fruits and nuts.

The Canadian government has proposed to the U.S. and Mexico that the USMCA be renewed for 16 years to avoid triggering the agreement’s mandatory annual review process. Under USMCA provisions, the three nations are scheduled to conduct their first joint review in July. The agreement, signed in 2018 and effective since July 2020, is set to expire in 2036.

U.S. officials are reportedly discussing whether to deploy nuclear weapons in additional NATO European countries. The U.S. already deploys nuclear-capable bombers in Belgium, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Turkey, and the UK, and has signaled openness to additional deployments.

Hawkish voices within the Federal Reserve are intensifying. Cleveland Fed President Harker said while maintaining current rates remains appropriate, persistent inflation data may soon necessitate policy action — including potential further rate hikes — to counter elevated inflation risks.

Labor market resilience continues to bolster Fed hawkish expectations. U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics data showed job openings rose to 7.62 million in April, the highest level in two years and well above the 6.87 million forecast. Layoffs fell to 1.692 million, significantly below expectations.

The European Union plans to impose strict eligibility criteria for cloud computing services in key public procurement projects, potentially excluding Amazon, Microsoft, and Google. The proposal aims to reduce EU dependence on U.S. tech products and promote domestic industry development.

Eurostat preliminary data showed euro area CPI rose 3.2% year-over-year in May, up from 3% in April, reinforcing market expectations of a modest ECB rate hike in June.

South Korea’s central bank is poised to raise rates. Statistics Korea reported that May CPI rose 3.1% year-over-year, the largest increase since March 2024. Core CPI increased 2.5%, the highest since February 2024.

The World Meteorological Organization released a report indicating most climate models predict this El Niño will develop into at least a moderate event and may intensify further. Seasonal forecasts suggest most regions will experience above-average temperatures in the coming months.

Global Equity Markets

U.S. major indices closed slightly higher: the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.45% to 51,307.79, the S&P 500 gained 0.13% to 7,609.78, and the Nasdaq Composite edged up 0.03% to 27,093.90 — all closing at record highs. Cisco rose over 5%, Caterpillar gained more than 5%, leading the Dow. The Wind US Magnificent Seven Index declined 1.05%, with Microsoft down over 4% and Google down nearly 4%. The Nasdaq China Golden Dragon Index rose 1.83%, with Canadian Solar up over 12% and Hesai Group up over 8%. Traders closely monitored developments in U.S.-Iran relations and major tech stock performance.

European indices closed higher: Germany’s DAX rose 0.48% to 25,124.17, France’s CAC 40 gained 0.77% to 8,209.09, and the UK’s FTSE 100 climbed 0.33% to 10,373.51. Progress in ceasefire negotiations between Iran and the U.S. lifted risk appetite, but Brent crude breached $95 per barrel, maintaining upward pressure on inflation expectations.

Asia-Pacific markets closed mixed. South Korea’s KOSPI swung sharply — initially surging before plunging more than 3% — and ultimately closed up 0.15% at 8,801.49, setting a new record. South Korea’s total market capitalization surpassed India’s, becoming the world’s sixth-largest stock market. Japan’s Nikkei 225 fell 0.3% to 66,734.24. India’s SENSEX rose 0.52% to 74,649.84.

Andrew Left, founder of the renowned short-selling firm Citron Research, was convicted by a U.S. court of stock manipulation and may face decades in prison. The case could mark a landmark moment for short-sellers globally and reshape regulatory boundaries for short reports and market commentary.

Data from India’s National Securities Depository Limited showed that as of June 1, foreign institutional investors’ net investment in Indian equities totaled 7.3 trillion rupees — the lowest since 2016. Global fund ownership in Indian equities has declined from nearly 20% a decade ago to 15% today.

Bonds

China’s interbank bond market traded in a narrow range, with yields on major government bonds moving slightly in different directions. Treasury futures rallied slightly at the close, with the 10-year contract up 0.06%. Liquidity conditions returned to ample levels as the PBOC conducted 200 million yuan in reverse repo operations, resulting in a net withdrawal of 248.8 billion yuan.

U.S. Treasury yields were mixed: the 2-year yield rose 1.24 basis points to 4.039%, the 3-year yield rose 0.59 basis points to 4.085%, the 5-year yield held steady at 4.163%, the 10-year yield fell 1.18 basis points to 4.439%, and the 30-year yield declined 1.21 basis points to 4.957%.

Commodities

U.S. crude oil futures rose 1.33% to $93.39 per barrel; Brent crude gained 1.01% to $95.94 per barrel. U.S. API crude inventories fell sharply, exceeding market expectations, while geopolitical tensions in the Middle East — including Iran’s suspension of U.S.-Iran talks and its plan to block the Strait of Hormuz — heightened concerns over supply disruptions. The strait handles roughly 20% of global oil shipments, and global crude inventories remain at low levels, jointly supporting higher prices.

International precious metals futures generally rose: COMEX gold futures gained 0.29% to $4,519.20 per ounce, and COMEX silver futures rose 0.25% to $75.44 per ounce. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East lifted safe-haven demand, while rising U.S. and European rate expectations weighed on market sentiment, creating a tug-of-war.

Most base metals on the LME rose: LME tin climbed 2.88% to $58,280 per ton, LME lead gained 1.52% to $2,037 per ton, LME zinc rose 1.48% to $3,631 per ton, LME aluminum advanced 1.20% to $3,760.50 per ton, LME copper increased 1.10% to $13,983.50 per ton, while LME nickel edged down 0.19% to $19,215 per ton.

The European Central Bank reported that as of the end of 2025, gold accounted for 27% of global official reserve assets, surpassing U.S. Treasuries, which fell to 22%. Gold has become the world’s largest official reserve asset.

Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) revealed plans to construct a refined products pipeline to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. The company is also exploring a new east-west crude oil trunk line that would allow other Gulf producers to route exports through UAE territory, avoiding the strait.

FX & Currency Markets

Onshore renminbi closed at 6.7622 against the U.S. dollar at 16:30, up 28 basis points from the previous close, and settled at 6.7650 in the night session. The PBOC’s mid-rate was set at 6.8187, a 20-basis-point depreciation from the prior day.

At the close of New York trading, the U.S. Dollar Index rose 0.03% to 99.22. Non-U.S. currencies were mixed: the euro fell 0.01% to 1.1631, the pound rose 0.07% to 1.3464, the Australian dollar rose 0.27% to 0.7180, the dollar rose 0.16% against the yen to 159.9295, the dollar fell 0.01% against the Canadian dollar to 1.3839, the dollar rose 0.08% against the Swiss franc to 0.7873, and the offshore renminbi gained 23 basis points to 6.7629.

Japan’s authorities issued a strong signal after record-sized foreign exchange interventions proved ineffective. Finance Minister Kato said the government stands ready to take appropriate action on exchange rates if necessary.

Photonics names back 

$AAOI $LITE led yesterday and today we saw other names like $GLW $COHR have strong days as well 

Like the look of some other names into tomorrow as well in this basket like $VIAV https://t.co/BuxbmFBvso

$lwlg Luminary 2회차 발간함

내용은 뭐 새로운 건 없음. 

PDK 1.1, 마이클 베스트랑 IP 자문계약한거 리마인드 정도..

단기 행사 일정으로는, 

1. Photonics North
- CTO 아레프 초우더리가 6월 4일 업계 기조 강연 진행

2. IEEE 광 인터커넥트 및 패키징(OIP) 컨퍼런스(6월 15~17일)
- Director of Reliability인 마노지 만자레가 6월 16일 발표 예정

그리고, Semiconductor Leadership Podcast에도 출연한다고 함. 

반도체 업계가 실리콘 포토닉스가 400G 이상의 광 네트워킹과 차세대 AI 연결을 어떻게 구현할 수 있는지, 그리고 더 낮은 전력 소비와 더 높은 집적도로 더 높은 대역폭을 제공하는 데 필요한 요소는 무엇인지에 점점 더 집중하고 있는 것과 관련하여, 

$lwlg SVP of Sales and Marketing인 로버트 블룸이 ISIG CEO 살라 나스리가 진행하는 반도체 리더십 팟캐스트 에 게스트로 출연하여 이 주제들을 심도 있게 다룰 예정. 

뭔가 좀 나와야 한다 $lwlg...

Original Air Date:  02.JUN.2026
8:00 PM EST

#1202 //  THE AI RACE - LIVE
In this first episode of The AI Race, we cut through the noise to deliver a clear-eyed survey of where artificial intelligence actually stands in 2026. We examine the real capabilities and hard limitations of current systems, the massive infrastructure and energy demands driving the buildout, and the technical paths — including photonics — that could help ease those pressures. We also explore the global competition shaping this technology, how narratives around AI and data centers are being shaped and amplified, and the serious questions of economic impact, power concentration, and governance that will determine who benefits. Along the way, we separate legitimate concerns from exaggerated fears while giving voice to the real experiences many had during recent years of information control. We close with late-breaking developments across geopolitics and global institutions, and a grounded look at what’s truly at stake as this race accelerates.
#QBits

text:
the alpha corner is gold if you ask me 👀 (i'm obviously biased)

I built the daily brief because I was tired of reading the same AI take 40 times a day and still not knowing where the buildout was actually tightening.

So now every morning it goes through the whole bottleneck book, memory, packaging, photonics, power, grid, cooling, and flags what's coiled before it's obvious. great way to synthetise everything and find where the pressure is.

have a look for yourself!

text:
The AI infrastructure buildout is barely in its first innings.

Everyone on here is trying to figure out where in the stack they want to be.

Power. Compute. HPC DCs. Photonics. Memory. Networking. Cooling. Models/Apps.

Some of those layers I understand well. Some I understand just enough to be dangerous.

That is why I keep coming back to the most physical parts of the stack.

Land. Power. Buildings. Capacity. Hyperscalers and AI labs who need more compute than the world can currently provide.

That is $IREN to me.

The market spent years treating it like its old business, and probably still does to some extent until AI revenue really shows up.

I think the real story is power-backed infrastructure as compute becomes one of the scarcest resources on earth.

$DGXX is earlier, smaller and more execution heavy, but that is part of the appeal to me.

It feels closer to an early mini $NBIS style setup if they execute.

Same bet: compute demand is a 2 to 3 decade buildout.

The sheer deficit in compute gives me sleep at night comfort. That is why I have sized these names accordingly.

And dare I say it, I think this sector can 100x over the next few decades.

I think we are still early in figuring out who owns the real choke points.

People like @jiahanjimliu, @Agrippa_Inv and @FransBakker9812 are deeper in parts of this stack, but this is how I’m framing it.

user_name: アスナロDrive｜30代共働きのオルカン+α投資@宇宙関連勉強中
text:
■「コンポーネント企業」のニッチな独占構造
＜高い参入障壁と認証の壁＞
宇宙空間は、激しい温度変化、放射線、真空状態、そして打ち上げ時の凄まじい振動に耐えなければなりません。
一度「宇宙での稼働実績（Flight Heritage）」を得たコンポーネントは、競合が容易にリプレイスできないため、先行者が市場を独占する構造です。

＜主要プレイヤー＞
Mynaric（$MYV / ドイツ・米国）:
LEO衛星向け光通信ターミナルのパイオニア。
SpaceX、DARPA、Northrop Grummanなどに製品を供給するセクターの代表格。

CACI International（$CACI）:
SA Photonicsを買収し、米政府・防衛向けの超高信頼性レーザー通信コンポーネントで圧倒的なシェア。

国内の動向:
ソニーグループが「ソニー宇宙光通信（SSC）」を設立して民間LEO方向への量産化を狙っているほか、三菱電機やNECなどの重電大手が防衛省向けに強みを持っています。

text:
This bull market won't last forever.

You need to take advantage of it while it's here.

Trillion-dollar industries are unfolding before our eyes:
AI, semis, photonics, space, data centers, energy, robotics, nuclear, drones, quantum.

The scale of change is massive, and the potential is enormous.

This bull markets is creating life-changing opportunities.

So, focus on the leading sectors and stocks. Be aggressive in the right areas, but don't get reckless. Always manage risk.

Moments like these are rare.

This is where fortunes are made.

Don't waste it.

text:
$SIVE and $AAPL.

Speculative post — no announced partnership exists. But the pattern is worth looking at.

People are noticing the same thing: Apple is quietly photonics-pilling its entire product stack.

→ Apple Watch — Long-rumored non-invasive glucose monitoring uses absorption spectroscopy. That requires tunable laser sources, not just standard VCSELs.

→ AirPods with cameras — The latest leak. Camera-enabled, always-on AI vision = depth sensors, time-of-flight components, low-power optical transmission.

→ iPhone LiDAR + Face ID — Already heavy on photonics. Next-gen sensing pushes toward higher resolution and longer range.

→ Vision Pro + future AR glasses
 — Eye tracking, depth perception, waveguide displays. All photonics-intensive.

→ Apple Intelligence servers — Apple is building its own AI infrastructure for Private Cloud Compute. Those datacenters need CPO, optical interconnects, External Light Sources — the exact stack $SIVE is built around.

Five surfaces. Five different photonics requirements. And Apple has historically diversified suppliers aggressively — they don't want permanent single-source dependency on Lumentum and Coherent.

Honest read of where $SIVE actually fits:
The simple VCSEL sensor work in current AirPods / Apple Watch is dominated by Lumentum and Coherent. That's not $SIVE's architecture.

But:
→ For LiDAR — $SIVE has a confirmed laser source program ramping Q4 2026.
→ For optical interconnects in Apple-built AI infrastructure — $SIVE sits in the same supply chain as Jabil and Ayar Labs.
→ For next-gen wearable sensing (glucose, advanced biometrics) — tunable InP-based lasers become more relevant as resolution and sensitivity requirements scale up. That IS $SIVE's architecture.

No claims of an existing relationship. Just an observation:

Apple is building photonics into five product lines at once. $SIVE happens to sit on the supply side of three of them.

🚨 NVIDIA JUST VALIDATED THE NEXT AI MEGA TREND: PHOTONICS.

The future is optical.
Data moving through LIGHT.

This could be massive for $SIVE.

AI data centers are hitting the wall:
power, bandwidth, latency, heat.

Copper is not enough anymore.

And $SIVE sits right in this theme with DFB lasers, optical interconnects, 1.6T transceiver exposure, Jabil collaboration, Ayar Labs partnership and CPO/NPO optionality.

NVIDIA pushing into photonics could wake up the entire market.

$SIVE the setup is getting very, very serious. $NVDA

those are double consensus, which is reasonable tbh as the street is WAY behind since most estimates are actually based on pre dust photonics revenue. 
Even Existing hyper scalers will get it there I think. 

But additional business including SiPho PICs, optics and cxl will get to $20s. 

Maybe I’m the outlier being 3-4x consensus. I felt like that when I was forecasting $50 for MU in 2026. Which proved conservative oddly, despite being 3x above consensus.

text:
Interesting article from Swedish media..

Nvidia is investing $6.5 billion into photonics.

Why? Data centers consume too much power. The fix: move data with light instead of electricity.

This is exactly the market $SIVE operates in.

Nvidia isn’t building this alone, it needs component suppliers. Sivers is one of the few publicly listed InP photonics companies in the world.

• Coherent — $2B + purchase commitments
• Lumentum — $2B
• Marvell — $2B (silicon photonics)
• Corning — $500M
• Ayar Labs — $500M Series 

Wouldn’t be suprised seeing $NVDA funding $SIVE too at some point for CPO build-out

Today’s photonics/AI infra tape is splitting hard: $DELL is the standout, up ~33%, and $AVGO is up roughly 5% as investors crowd into scaled AI server/networking winners. 

Meanwhile, the higher-beta optics names are cooling off, with $AAOI down about 6% and $AXTI down around 11% after big moves. 

$SIVE is still volatile but positive on the Stockholm tape, recently showing around +4% on the day - but down in the U.S. markets.

Let’s see how things play out! 

I’m still bullish on all. 🐂

text:
Nvidia just bet $6.5 BILLION on light-based data transfer to fix AI's biggest bottleneck ⚡

Photonics could replace copper wiring in data centers — faster, cooler, more efficient.

The AI infrastructure race is getting interesting. #AI #Nvidia #Tech #Innovation #DataCenters https://t.co/CAyldoD2Kp

Interesting upstream read through. CPO doesn’t integrate lasers into the package. Both Broadcom and NVIDIA use external pluggable laser modules (ELSFP standard) that feed light into the silicon photonics engines. Bailly uses 16 per switch; Quantum-X manages all 144 × 800G channels with just 18. ￼

10,000 Foxconn switch trays = ~160,000 ELSFP modules this year at Bailly-equivalent counts.

The ELSFP supply chain is a two-horse race. $LITE announced its largest PO ever for ELSFPs and $COHR announced one too. ￼ Both targeting volume into scale-up hardware by 2027.

AI Photonics

- $MRVL "CPO units [in the Market] from 1MM in 2025 to more than 100 MM by 2030"

- Foxconn: "We will ship around 10k CPO units this year and the shipment next year will grow several times"

text:
Nvidia has committed at least $6.5 billion since March 2026 to photonics companies as it pushes to replace copper-based data links with light-based infrastructure for AI systems that are straining existing capacity. The investment spree spans $2 billion stakes in Coherent and Lumentum tied to multiyear supply and capacity agreements, a $2 billion investment in Marvell Technology focused on custom chips and silicon photonics, participation in Ayar Labs’ $500 million Series E, and a $500 million investment in Corning to expand U.S. fiber manufacturing capacity. At GTC, CEO Jensen Huang said co-packaged optics are central to Nvidia’s Spectrum-X Ethernet platform, as the company bets that silicon photonics will be key to scaling AI compute while managing power constraints.

FOUNDER: Jensen Huang

AMOUNT: $6,500,000,000

#VentureCapital #NVidia #JensenHuang #TradedVC

https://t.co/7ALZf7Eb0a

Squeezed states of light represent one of the most fundamental concepts  in quantum optics. They play a crucial role in various fields of  science, quantum technology, and photonics, with applications in quantum  information processing, quantum computation, quantum sensing, and  gravitational wave detection. In this paper, we propose a plasmonic  waveguide for the generation of squeezed states via four-wave mixing.

text:
Sivers $SIVEF $SIVE just landed exactly the validation the thesis needed.

Its laser arrays are now integrated into $GFS ’s silicon photonics + SCALE co-packaged optics platform -> the reference designs the AI data center buildout is being built on.

This is the “light, not copper” shift made concrete. The lasers that move data inside the cluster don’t get the headlines. They get the design wins.

Still early innings but the chokepoint is real, and $SIVE just got named in it. 🔥
embedded_text:
Many people read the Sivers $SIVE/GlobalFoundries $GFS PR and see "AI optics."

I see something more: GF's SCALE platform is explicitly positioned as a CPO solution for OCI (Optical Scale-Up Initiative), whose founding members are: NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI https://t.co/ey0CeT74Wk

text:
$GFS - GLOBALFOUNDRIES CLOSED TWO AI DEALS IN ONE DAY — PROCESSOR IP AND SILICON-PHOTONICS LASERS

On Tuesday GlobalFoundries completed its acquisition of Synopsys' ARC processor-IP business — which, bolted onto MIPS, gives it a software-to-silicon stack aimed at physical AI — and separately announced a silicon-photonics partnership with Sivers to integrate laser arrays into its AI-data-center optical reference designs. Shares rose ~6%.

GF is repositioning from a commodity trailing-edge foundry into the connectivity and edge-AI layers — processor IP, photonics, and a U.S.-backed quantum foundry — where the multiple expansion lives, on top of the first dividend it declared last month.
